name: Couscous Beidaoui
recipeIngredient: 3 pounds lamb shanks, or lamb shoulder
20 sprigs parsley, washed & tied with string
10 sprigs cilantro, washed & tied with string
1/4 cup raisins, plumped in warm water and drained
recipeInstructions:
1. In a Dutch oven or large soup pot, heat the oil over medium-high heat. Brown the lamb on all sides for 5-6 minutes.
2. Add the onions and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
3. Stir in the tomatoes, parsley, cilantro, water, and bring to a rolling boil.
4. Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 15 minutes.
5. Lower the heat further and add saffron, ginger, carrots, turnips, and pumpkin. Simmer until the vegetables are tender, about 30-35 minutes.
6. Preheat the oven to 200°F (93°C).
7. Transfer carrots and pumpkin to a baking dish and keep warm in the oven.
8. Add cabbage and simmer covered for 25-30 minutes. Transfer to the oven and keep warm.
9. Discard parsley and cilantro. Add zucchini and garbanzo beans, then simmer for an additional 10 minutes.
10. Prepare couscous according to package directions. Stir in butter and fluff with a fork.
11. Serve by mounding couscous on a platter. Artfully arrange cooked vegetables around the lamb pieces. Sprinkle raisins over everything. Offer extra broth alongside.
(serves 6-8)
